AMR Resistance Burden vs Health Expenditure — Country vs High Income Countries (2004–2024)

AMR Data Challenge 2026 · Phase 3 Geographic Analysis · Nosocomial Neuroinvasive Bacterial AMR · Resistance: Pfizer ATLAS · Expenditure: WHO GHED via UNdata

Organism
Country
Resistance Type
Age Group
Legend
Left Axis — Resistance
Country resistance rate (%)
HIC weighted mean rate (%)
Excess resistance burden
50% crisis threshold
Right Axis — Expenditure
Country health exp. (% GDP)
HIC mean expenditure (% GDP)
Funding gap area
How to read this chart. This is a dual Y-axis chart plotting two independent variables on the same time axis (2004–2024) for direct visual comparison of resistance burden and healthcare investment.

Left Y-axis — Resistance Rate (%):
• Red solid line — selected country's MDR/XDR/PDR rate per year. Higher = more isolates resistant to multiple antibiotic classes that year.
• Blue dashed line — weighted mean resistance rate across all HIC countries — the benchmark achievable in well-resourced healthcare systems.
•  Pink shaded area — excess resistance burden (country minus HIC). Wider = greater disparity.
• Grey dotted line — 50% crisis threshold (ECDC AMR Threat Report 2022).

Right Y-axis — Health Expenditure (% of GDP):
• Green solid line (diamond markers) — selected country's health expenditure as % of GDP. WHO GHED via UNdata SYB68 (May 2025) — snapshots 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020, 2021, 2022 only.
• Purple dashed line — mean expenditure % of GDP across all HIC countries for those same years.
•  Green shaded area — funding gap: how much less the country spends vs HIC mean.

What to look for: When the green line (spending) goes down while the red line (resistance) goes up, this is visual evidence of the inverse relationship between healthcare investment and AMR burden. Countries with wide green shading AND wide pink shading face a structural double disadvantage — underfunded systems sustaining higher resistance.

Data sources: Resistance — Pfizer ATLAS 2004–2024. Expenditure — WHO GHED via UNdata SYB68 (May 2025). MDR/XDR/PDR — Magiorakos et al. CMI 2012;18:268–281. HIC — World Bank FY2026. Equity gap — Laxminarayan et al. Lancet Infect Dis 2013. Crisis threshold — ECDC AMR Threat Report 2022. Min. isolates — ≥10 per cell (WHO GLASS 2023).

Definitions: MDR — ≥3 antibiotic classes non-susceptible  |  XDR — ≤2 classes susceptible  |  PDR — 0 classes susceptible. Magiorakos et al. CMI 2012. HIC — World Bank FY2026.   Min. isolates — 10 (WHO GLASS 2023).   Expenditure data: WHO GHED snapshots 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020–2022. No interpolation applied.
